title: Your Worth Is Not Your Work
content: I spent years measuring myself by what I produced. My output. My achievements. My usefulness to others. Then I hit a wall. And I learned: your worth is not your work. Your job title, your productivity, your accomplishments - none of it defines your value as a human being. You matter because you exist. Period.

My usefulness to others. <break time="300ms"/>Then I hit a wall. <break time="200ms"/>And I learned: your worth is not your work. <break time="300ms"/>Your job title, your productivity, your accomplishments - none of it defines your value as a human being. <break time="200ms"/>You matter because you exist. teaching_point: Your value is not determined by external measures
